Pilsner Urquell is the world's first pale lager, created in 1842 in Plzeň, Czech Republic, and owned by Asahi Breweries. This 4.4% ABV golden beer features Saaz hops and soft water. It has a golden hue, creamy head, and a balanced flavor of sweet malt and herbal, slightly woody bitterness from Saaz hops. It is still produced in Plzeň using traditional methods, including triple decoction, ensuring its characteristic flavor. Good value from seller for case of 6 glass bottles 330ml each. Only this Pilsner Urquell delivers such unique taste from other beers for my taste buds!! Perfect to pair with meaty dishes as well as Asian cuisine because of its dryness. Long expiry till 7/26!

It is hopped with Saaz hops, a noble hop variety which is a key element in its flavour profile, as is the use of soft water. title: Hoegaarden White Original Belgian Witbier Beer Multipack 330ml Cans 24-Pack
detail_text: The Original Belgian Wheat Beer, Anno 1445 Almost 600 years ago in the Belgian village of Hoegaarden®, a band of monks experimented by adding oddball botanicals from halfway around the world to their traditional wheat beer. Our recipe, inspired by their creation, is a beer with a refreshing flavor and captivating aroma, unlike any in the world. You might call it divine inspiration… we call it Hoegaarden® FUNCTIONALITY Hoegaarden® is a smooth and easy-to-drink beer. Naturally low bitterness (IBUs), Hoegaarden® is a uniquely refreshing and easy-to-drink beer, especially for beer drinkers taking a step up from mainstream lagers. You’ll discover surprising ingredients: wheat, Curaçao orange peel, and coriander. The Hoegaarden® brewing process is unique and extremely complex. CHARACTERISTICS Hoegaarden® White – High fermentation (at 25°C); pasteurised in the bottle after main fermentation. – Second fermentation in bottle/barrel. – Unfiltered. Alcohol level bottle/barrel: Alc. 4.9% vol./ Alc. 4.8% vol. Density: 11.7° Plato Looks: Natural cloudiness, frosted look, dense white head. Taste: Especially sweet and fruity to the palate, soon turning to a slightly bitter after-taste. Its special ingredients and intense and aroma create an invigorating and extremely refreshing beer. Delicious! Hoegaarden® Rosée Full, fruity raspberry flavour with subtle floral and woody notes. Its refreshing taste is sweet and slightly sour with low bitterness and nearly no after bitterness. – Typical Hoegaarden® yeast strain – Top fermented beer at Alc. 3% vol. – Non filtered, non refermented – Very hazy beer with a vivid raspberry colour Ingredients: Barley malt, wheat, hops, coriander, Curaçao orange peel, Hoegaarden® yeast, sugar, sweeteners, juices (raspberry, strawberry, apple, elderberry) and natural aroma Download PDF
detail_html:
The Original Belgian Wheat Beer, Anno 1445

Almost 600 years ago in the Belgian village of Hoegaarden®, a band of monks experimented by adding oddball botanicals from halfway around the world to their traditional wheat beer. Our recipe, inspired by their creation, is a beer with a refreshing flavor and captivating aroma, unlike any in the world. You might call it divine inspiration… we call it Hoegaarden®

FUNCTIONALITY

Hoegaarden® is a smooth and easy-to-drink beer. Naturally low bitterness (IBUs), Hoegaarden® is a uniquely refreshing and easy-to-drink beer, especially for beer drinkers taking a step up from mainstream lagers. You’ll discover surprising ingredients: wheat, Curaçao orange peel, and coriander. The Hoegaarden® brewing process is unique and extremely complex.

CHARACTERISTICS

Hoegaarden® White

– High fermentation (at 25°C); pasteurised in the bottle after main fermentation.

– Second fermentation in bottle/barrel.

– Unfiltered.

Alcohol level bottle/barrel: Alc. 4.9% vol./ Alc. 4.8% vol.

Density: 11.7° Plato

Looks: Natural cloudiness, frosted look, dense white head.

Taste:
